claims.claim: Najib convicted on all 25 charges Dec 26 2025.
Verdict: 4 Section 23(1) MACC + 21 AMLATFPUAA charges; 15 years jail + RM11.38B fine.
claims.correction: Cleaner formulation: 15 years imprisonment, RM11.38B fine on abuse-of-power counts, plus separate RM2.081B recoverable sum ordered under money-laundering counts.

claims.claim: Judge declared four Saudi donation letters forgeries.
claims.source: The Edge + Star + FMT + NST 26 Dec 2025 (Reuters used 'likely forged')
Malay Mail + The Star + Bernama + Reuters 30 Dec 2025

claims.claim: RM24.46B in 1MDB debt still owed through 2039.
claims.source: Only RM9.02B IMTN explicitly runs to 2039; RM15.44B are shareholder advances from MOF/MOF Inc with no explicit 2039 horizon.
claims.correction: RM24.46B outstanding end-Sept 2025, of which RM9.02B IMTN runs to 2039. Full RM24.46B should not be described as all owed through 2039.

claims.claim: RM42.17B debt service paid = more than a third of 2026 education budget.
claims.source: Bernama MOF reply 13 Aug 2025 + Budget 2026 para 187
claims.correction: If comparison is the outstanding (RM24.46B) figure, ratio is 36.95% = 'more than a third'.

claims.claim: One side has 76 witnesses and forged Saudi letters.
claims.source: Al Jazeera: 76 total. The Edge verdict microsite: 50 prosecution + 26 defence.
claims.correction: 'The trial heard 76 witnesses in total, and the court rejected the Arab-donation letters as forged.'

claims.claim: A political financing bill already exists, no party in power has moved to pass it.
claims.source: APPG private member's bill 13 Jul 2022 (IDEAS). Malaysian Bar 23 Jun 2022. IDEAS 3 Apr 2026: still no timeline.
claims.correction: Draft exists; as of 20 Apr 2026 no political financing bill tabled or passed.

claims.claim: PH, BN, PN — every party runs on patronage. None will dismantle it voluntarily.
claims.source: Academic lit supports entrenched patronage; no primary source for the absolute 'every party' formulation.
claims.correction: Safer: 'Patronage and clientelist politics remain deeply embedded; reform has repeatedly stalled.'

claims.claim: B40 single mothers bear the sharpest cost / B40 households — disproportionately women-led — pay.
claims.source: Generic OECD 2012 / World Bank 2023. No Malaysia-specific primary evidence tying 1MDB servicing to B40 single mothers as sharpest-hit.
claims.correction: Support only as broad hypothesis, not established Malaysia-specific fact.

claims.claim: Najib is convicted. Patronage itself is untouched.
claims.source: Conviction verified. IDEAS 3 Apr 2026 confirms reform stasis. But post-1MDB governance changes in 2016 included 1MDB Board dissolution and removal of Article 117 (MOF release 31 May 2016).
claims.correction: Tighter: 'Najib is convicted, but major structural reform — especially political financing reform — remains incomplete.'

cardinal_sin_flags.why: Only RM9.02B IMTN runs to 2039; RM15.44B shareholder advances have no explicit 2039 horizon.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: B40 single mothers bear the sharpest cost.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No Malaysia-specific primary evidence on 1MDB servicing incidence by household type.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: PH, BN, PN — every party runs on patronage.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Absolute totalising statement not verified by primary source.
No primary source for this as factual descriptor; reads as rhetoric.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Najib is convicted. Patronage itself is untouched.

omitted_facts: RM31.2B recovered assets as of Sept 2025 (Bernama 27 Nov 2025).
RM24.46B breakdown: RM9.02B IMTN + RM15.44B MOF shareholder advances.
Post-1MDB 2016 governance changes: Board dissolution, Article 117 removal.
76 witnesses is total (50 prosecution + 26 defence), not one side.
Source-level confusion combining RM11.38B fine with RM2.081B recoverable sum.
overall_assessment: Strongest on verdict and debt headline — those check out. Main weakness is overreach: several lines convert arguable systemic critique into statements phrased as settled fact. Reliable on verdict + debt; materially weaker on social/political framing.
